summary_strategy: Position Conner as a reliability-focused security engineer who delivers production-grade tooling and automation for public-sector systems, emphasizing operational rigor, IAM/DevSecOps integration, and auditable AI-augmented SecOps
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format to group skills into high-impact categories aligned with Granicus’s public-sector priorities: Security Engineering & Automation, Cloud/Platform & Integrations, and Security Data/Analytics/AI — each line must reflect concrete actions (e.g., 'vulnerability remediation automation', 'Microsoft Graph API workflows') without embellishment

experience_focus.must_highlight: production-grade tooling reliability, IAM/DevSecOps integration, auditable AI-augmented SecOps
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: startup-style language (e.g., 'pioneered', 'disruptive'), speculative metrics, vague claims like 'best-in-class' or 'industry-leading'

risk_controls: Replace all em dashes with commas or parentheses
Never invent metrics beyond those explicitly listed in safe_metrics_to_keep
Avoid phrases like 'cutting-edge', 'best-in-class', or subjective superlatives; use factual descriptors (e.g., 'production-grade', 'operational reliability')
Do not add projects, tools, or responsibilities absent from source material
